meta.title: Getting Started
meta.description: How to approach this course, who it's for, and what you'll learn at each level.

blocks.content:
## Welcome to Claude Code Mastery

This course teaches you to use Claude Code — Anthropic's command-line AI agent — to solve real problems in your work and life. It is designed for **anyone**, not just software developers. Whether you write code, manage projects, do research, write documents, or organize information, Claude Code can transform how you work.

But only if you understand how to think with it.

Most people treat Claude Code like a chatbot: they type a question, get an answer, and move on. That works, but it misses 90% of what Claude Code can do. Claude Code is an **autonomous agent** — it can read files, edit documents, run commands, search your projects, and execute multi-step plans. The difference between a novice and an expert is not feature knowledge. It is **mental models** — patterns of thought that make the right action obvious.

This course teaches those mental models.

## How This Course Works

Each level introduces a **mental model** — a new way of thinking about your collaboration with Claude Code. Then it teaches the features and techniques that flow from that model. By the end of each level, you will not just know what a feature does. You will know *when and why* to reach for it.

| Level | Name | Mental Model |
| :-- | :-- | :-- |
| 1 | The Loop | Claude Code runs a gather→act→verify loop. You manage the loop. |
| 2 | The Briefing | You are writing a briefing for a capable colleague. Quality in, quality out. |
| 3 | The Dashboard | Knowing what tools exist helps you steer and unlocks new possibilities. |
| 4 | The Budget | Context is finite. Managing it is the single biggest skill gap. |
| 5 | The Blueprint | Planning before building prevents the most expensive mistakes. |
| 6 | The Workshop | You can build your own tools, automations, and integrations. |
| 7 | The Fleet | Coordinate multiple parallel Claude sessions for transformative speed. |
| 8 | The Intuition | Mastery is intuition for the collaboration. The tool becomes invisible. |

## Each Module Follows a Learning Cycle

1. **Predict** — Before reading, you will be asked what you think or expect. This activates your prior knowledge.
2. **Learn** — Core content with explanations, examples, and tables.
3. **Practice** — Hands-on exercises you do in your own Claude Code session.
4. **Reflect** — Questions that help you internalize the concepts.
5. **Connect** — Links to the next module and to your real-world work.

## Choosing Your Path

### If you have never used Claude Code
Start at **Level 1**. It covers installation, first use, and the foundational mental model.

### If you already use Claude Code casually
Skim Level 1, then start at **Level 2: The Briefing**. This is where most people's biggest improvement comes from.

### If you are an experienced Claude Code user
Jump to **Level 4: The Budget**. Context management is where intermediate users plateau, and Levels 4-6 contain the insights that separate casual users from power users.

### If you are technical and want to extend Claude Code
Start at **Level 6: The Workshop** for custom skills, hooks, agents, and MCP integrations.

## Tips for Effective Learning

- **Do the exercises in your own Claude Code session.** Reading about Claude Code is like reading about swimming. You have to get in the water.
- **Do not skip the prediction prompts.** Forming a guess before reading — even a wrong one — dramatically improves retention.
- **Space your learning.** Two modules per session with breaks is better than powering through five at once.
- **Apply to your real work immediately.** After each module, try the technique on something you actually need to do. This is where learning becomes permanent.
